question: What certification does this kit prepare you for?
answer: Claude Certified Architect – Foundations (CCA-F), Anthropic's first official Claude technical certification [OFFICIAL]

question: Who is the CCA-F aimed at?
answer: Solution architects building production applications with Claude [OFFICIAL]

question: What platform delivers the CCA-F exam?
answer: Anthropic Academy on Skilljar (anthropic.skilljar.com) [OFFICIAL]

question: How do you get exam access at launch?
answer: Join the (free) Claude Partner Network, then request access via Skilljar [OFFICIAL]

question: Reported exam format?
answer: ~60 scenario-based multiple-choice questions, 120 minutes [COMMUNITY]

question: Reported passing score?
answer: ~720 on a scaled 100–1000 scale [COMMUNITY]

question: Reported cost per attempt?
answer: ~$99 (waived for first 5,000 partner employees) [COMMUNITY]

question: The five reported exam domains?
answer: Agentic Architecture; Prompt Engineering & Structured Output; Tool Design & MCP; Context & Reliability; Claude Code Configuration [COMMUNITY]

question: Difference between a workflow and an agent?
answer: Workflow = LLM/tools on predefined code paths; agent = LLM dynamically directs its own process in a loop

question: The guiding design principle for agents?
answer: Use the simplest solution that works; add complexity only when it demonstrably improves outcomes

question: When do you need an agent rather than a workflow?
answer: When the path can't be predicted in advance and the model must adapt its own steps

question: Name the five workflow patterns.
answer: Prompt chaining, routing, parallelization, orchestrator–workers, evaluator–optimizer

question: What is the routing pattern best for?
answer: Distinct input categories handled better by specialized prompts/models

question: Two flavors of parallelization?
answer: Sectioning (independent subtasks) and voting (same task multiple times)

question: How do orchestrator–workers differ from parallelization?
answer: The orchestrator dynamically decides the subtasks; parallelization uses predefined ones

question: When is evaluator–optimizer a good fit?
answer: Clear eval criteria exist and iteration measurably improves the output

question: The three capabilities of the agentic loop?
answer: Gather context, take action, verify work (repeat until done)

question: Why must agents have stop conditions?
answer: Without max iterations/budget/explicit done, they can loop forever or burn budget

question: Default: single-agent or multi-agent?
answer: Single agent with good tools; use multi-agent only for parallel breadth or separation of concerns

question: When is multi-agent worth ~15x the tokens?
answer: Broad, open-ended, parallelizable tasks (e.g., research across many directions)

question: #1 failure mode of subagent delegation?
answer: Vague delegation — subagents need a specific objective, scope, tools, and output format

question: How should worker results return to the orchestrator?
answer: As condensed, structured summaries (not raw dumps) to keep context clean

question: How does a long-running agent survive a context reset?
answer: Persist plan/state to external storage and reload just-in-time; support session resumption

question: Hooks vs prompts rule?
answer: Soft guidance → prompt; hard requirement that must always happen → deterministic hook/code

question: First rung of the prompt-engineering ladder?
answer: Be clear, direct, and detailed (treat Claude like a brilliant new hire)

question: Most powerful lever for matching a format or style?
answer: Few-shot examples (multishot prompting)

question: Three properties of good few-shot examples?
answer: Relevant, diverse, and clearly delimited

question: Risk if all examples share the same answer?
answer: The model learns a bias and over-predicts that answer

question: When should you NOT use chain-of-thought?
answer: Simple single-step tasks where latency/cost matter

question: How to prompt an extended-thinking model on a hard task?
answer: Give the goal and room to think; don't micromanage each step

question: Most reliable way to get schema-valid JSON?
answer: Define a tool whose input_schema is your output schema and force the tool call; then validate

question: What does prefilling the assistant turn do?
answer: Controls the start/format (e.g., prefill '{' to force JSON and skip preamble)

question: Why use XML tags in prompts?
answer: To separate instructions, context, examples, and input so the model doesn't confuse them

question: Where do durable role/policy instructions go?
answer: The system prompt (also maximizes prompt-cache hits)

question: Three tactics to reduce hallucination in document Q&A?
answer: Allow 'not found', answer only from the document, and quote supporting text/cite sources

question: How do you actually know prompt A beats prompt B?
answer: An eval set with graders; change one variable at a time and measure

question: In the tool-use loop, who executes the tool?
answer: Your application — the model only emits a tool_use intent; you return a tool_result

question: Why is the tool description so important?
answer: It functions as a prompt; under-specified descriptions are the top cause of tool misuse

question: Four properties of a well-designed tool?
answer: Clear name, rich description, well-typed input_schema, concise useful results

question: Fix for the model picking the wrong tool between two similar ones?
answer: Consolidate/clarify tools and specify exactly when to use each in the descriptions

question: What does tool_choice control?
answer: Whether/which tool is used: auto, any (force some tool), a specific tool, or none

question: What is MCP, in one line?
answer: An open standard for connecting AI apps to external tools/data/prompts — a 'USB-C port for AI'

question: MCP's three core primitives?
answer: Tools (model-controlled), Resources (app-controlled), Prompts (user-controlled)

question: Which MCP primitive is model-controlled?
answer: Tools

question: Which MCP primitive is user-controlled?
answer: Prompts (e.g., surfaced as slash commands)

question: Which MCP primitive is application-controlled?
answer: Resources (read-only context loaded by URI)

question: Define MCP host, client, server.
answer: Host = the AI app the user uses; client = 1:1 connector inside the host; server = program exposing tools/resources/prompts

question: Local vs remote MCP transports?
answer: stdio for local; HTTP/SSE (streamable) for remote

question: Main reason to use MCP over hardcoded tools?
answer: Write the server once and reuse it across any MCP-compatible host (interoperability)

question: Two security practices for a remote MCP server?
answer: Require auth and validate/scope inputs (treat inputs as untrusted; use roots to limit filesystem)

question: Why return IDs instead of huge payloads from a tool?
answer: Concise results conserve context tokens and keep the agent reliable over long sessions

question: What is context engineering?
answer: Curating the entire set of tokens so the model has the right info, in the right form, at the right time

question: What is 'context rot'?
answer: Performance degrading as irrelevant or excess tokens dilute the model's attention

question: What is just-in-time retrieval?
answer: Fetching information when needed rather than pre-stuffing everything into the context window

question: Where do you place a prompt-cache breakpoint?
answer: After the stable prefix (system prompt, tools, fixed context); variable content comes last

question: Why must cached content come first and be identical?
answer: Anything before the breakpoint must be byte-identical to hit the cache

question: Prompt caching cost model?
answer: Cache writes cost slightly more than normal tokens; cache reads are much cheaper and faster

question: When is prompt caching clearly worth it?
answer: When a large stable prefix is reused across many calls (e.g., agent loops, doc Q&A)

question: How do you handle a conversation exceeding the window?
answer: Compact/summarize older turns (keep decisions/open tasks) and offload to external memory

question: Two cost levers for a high-volume, latency-insensitive job?
answer: Message Batches API + prompt caching of the fixed instruction/schema prefix

question: Best practice for rate-limit/transient API errors?
answer: Retry with exponential backoff; ensure idempotency on side-effecting tools

question: Two ways to maximize correctness when cost is secondary?
answer: Lower temperature and use self-consistency (sample N, take the majority)

question: Biggest reliability risk in a RAG system?
answer: Poor retrieval — garbage chunks in means garbage answers out

question: Sensible model selection strategy?
answer: Small/fast model for routing/classification; strong model for hard reasoning; route between them

question: What is Claude Code?
answer: A terminal-based agentic coding tool that runs the agentic loop over your codebase; also usable headless

question: Which config file is committed and shared with the team?
answer: .claude/settings.json (project scope)

question: What is .claude/settings.local.json?
answer: Personal, gitignored overrides for the project

question: Highest-authority Claude Code config layer?
answer: Enterprise/managed policy (admins; can't be overridden by users)

question: What is CLAUDE.md for?
answer: Persistent project/user context (conventions, architecture, commands) auto-loaded into context

question: What must never go in CLAUDE.md?
answer: Secrets/API keys

question: What are Claude Code hooks?
answer: Shell commands that run automatically at lifecycle events for deterministic control

question: Which hook event can block an action before it runs?
answer: PreToolUse

question: Common use of a PostToolUse hook?
answer: Auto-run formatter/linter/tests after an edit

question: Three things a subagent gets that isolate it?
answer: Its own context window, its own system prompt, and a restricted toolset

question: What is an Agent Skill?
answer: A folder with SKILL.md (instructions + metadata) plus optional scripts, auto-applied when relevant

question: Why are Skills better than a giant CLAUDE.md for some knowledge?
answer: They're progressively loaded only when the task matches, saving context

question: How do MCP prompts appear in Claude Code?
answer: As slash commands the user can invoke

question: What's mandatory for headless Claude Code in CI?
answer: Least-privilege permissions + enforcement hooks + logging (no human to approve)

question: Where are subagents defined in a project?
answer: As markdown files (e.g., .claude/agents/) with when-to-use descriptions
